energy.oneliner: Energy sector remains strongly bullish: Hormuz-driven supply collapse, surging inflation, and relentless inventory draws

inflation_linkage.summary: The US CPI energy index surged 3.8% month-over-month in April 2026 and 17.9% year-over-year, signalling strongly rising energy inflation and a clear sector tailwind.

eia_inventory.summary: EIA reported a draw of 8.0 million barrels in U.S. commercial crude oil inventories for the week ending May 29, 2026, bringing stockpiles to 433.7 million barrels (bullish).

industrials.oneliner: Industrials momentum broadens on surging PMI and freight, but soft capex and flat ASX price action temper conviction.

manufacturing_pmi.summary: The ISM Manufacturing PMI rose to 54.0 in May 2026, up 1.3 points from April's 52.7 reading and its highest level since May 2022, per Trading Economics / ISM data released June 2, 2026.

new_orders.summary: The ISM Manufacturing New Orders sub-index registered 56.8% in May 2026, up 2.7 percentage points from April's 54.1%, marking the fifth consecutive month of expansion.

semiconductors.oneliner: Strong AI/HBM demand supercycle drives semiconductor upside, offset by escalating Taiwan Strait geopolitical risk.

materials.oneliner: Strong China PMI and surging copper lift ASX Materials bullishly, though a firming USD and retreating gold temper upside

china_pmi.summary: China's Caixin Manufacturing PMI for May 2026 came in at 51.8, beating forecasts and marking the second consecutive above-consensus reading.

copper_price.summary: LME copper spot price is approximately $13,965/tonne as of June 2, 2026, up ~14.75% over the past 30 days, well above the >3% upside threshold.

asx_materials.summary: The S&P/ASX 200 Materials Index (XMJ/^AXMJ) is currently around 23,788 (intraday, June 3 2026), and surged approximately +10.51% in May 2026, well above the +3% threshold for a bullish signal.

credit_spreads.summary: US HY OAS tightened to ~2.72% in May 2026, sitting near multi-decade lows and well below its 20-year average of ~4.9%, indicating spreads are firmly tightening/compressed.

asx_banks.summary: The ASX 200 Banks Index (XBK) is currently at approximately 3,931.90, reflecting a 1-month return of roughly -1.75% (from ~4,002.10), which falls within the flat range.
